reviewNotes.note: curl3 signature is `curl3(vec3 p, int octaves)` — confirmed in noise3d.glsl. The key_algorithm snippet uses correct signature. However, `uTime * 0.00005` is extremely slow — at 60fps this moves ~0.003/sec. Verify this produces visible motion or bump to ~0.0002.
COMMON_UNIFORMS already includes uFogNear and uFogFar. The plan correctly identifies only uFlowScale as a new uniform beyond the common set.

Elliptical gl_PointCoord stretching works but the sprite remains square — elongation is purely visual within the square bounds. For strong elongation (vElongation > 0.7), the ellipse will be clipped by the square sprite edges. Consider also scaling gl_PointSize up when elongation is high to compensate, or clamping vElongation to avoid clipping artifacts.

Registering FLOWRIBBON_ATTRIBUTES as identical to RIBBONFIELD_ATTRIBUTES is fine but consider whether a separate export is needed at all — could reuse RIBBONFIELD_ATTRIBUTES directly in step 4. A separate export is acceptable for clarity and future divergence.

The streamline pre-advection approach (seed points, advect along curl3 on CPU) is a good differentiator from ribbonField's helical band distribution. Note that curl3 must be reimplemented in JS for CPU-side streamline generation — the GLSL version in noise3d.glsl won't be available. Ensure the JS snoise/fbm3/curl3 implementation matches the GLSL version for visual coherence.
QualityProfile interface currently has no maxFlowRibbonPoints. Values 200/700/1600 mirror maxRibbonPoints exactly — appropriate given similar shader complexity.

Current modes array has 6 entries (particles, ribbon, pointcloud, crystal, microgeometry, wirepolyhedra). Adding flowribbon as 7th is straightforward. ModeManager selects via seeded RNG so no ordering concerns.
Existing health gate from US-048 will catch shader compile errors automatically. Build step validates TypeScript. Plan coverage is adequate.

riskAssessment: Plan risks are well-identified. The CPU-side curl3 reimplementation for streamline seeding (step 4) is the most complex piece not explicitly called out — ensure it's budgeted. Overall complexity assessment of 'medium' is accurate.
verdict: Approved. Plan is well-structured, follows established codebase patterns, and covers all acceptance criteria. Minor notes on time scaling and sprite clipping are non-blocking.
